channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: DA requests investigation into alleged irregularities in eThekwini soup kitchen programme
item.description: The Democratic Alliance (DA) in eThekwini has formally requested the Public Protector to investigate alleged irregularities in the municipality’s soup kitchen programme. The concern centres on claims ...

item.title: Tanzania MPs Urge Proper Use of Road Fund and Timely Contractor Payments
item.description: In Dodoma, Tanzanian MPs are stressing the importance of protecting the Road Fund revenues to ensure proper infrastructure development. They are warning the government that diverting these funds or de...

item.title: Practical Ways to Make Screen Time Healthier and More Purposeful
item.description: The average adult in the UK spends roughly 7.5 hours a day on screens, spanning phones, laptops, consoles and TVs. While screen time is often criticised, experts stress that not all usage is harmful. ...

item.title: European Women Embrace Japanese Kimonos During the Early 1900s
item.description: In the early 1900s, Europe experienced a cultural phenomenon known as Japonisme, a fascination with Japanese art, fashion, and culture. Among the high society of the Belle Époque, the Japanese kimono ...

item.title: Indian factory workers provide AI training data through wearable cameras
item.description: In April, a video went viral showing Indian textile workers at a Gurugram factory wearing head-mounted cameras during their shifts. The footage captured their daily work activities without their conse...

item.title: Prominent entertainer and two others in court over teen shooting and kidnapping
item.description: Mlindelwa “Man B” Tyekana, a well-known businessman and entertainer, appeared in the KuGompo City Magistrate’s Court for his first bail hearing in connection with a serious teen shooting case. He face...

item.title: Concerns Raised Over Environmental and Planning Impacts of New AI Data Centres in Australia
item.description: Tech companies are rapidly investing in AI data centres across Australia, with the nation becoming the second-largest destination for such projects globally. However, this rapid growth has sparked con...

item.title: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio to visit India for Quad Foreign Ministers’ meeting
item.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio is scheduled to visit India for the Quad Foreign Ministers’ meeting in New Delhi on 26 May 2026. US Ambassador to India, Sergio Gor, expressed his excitement on socia...

item.title: How Much Income Tax Australians Actually Pay in 2025
item.description: Ahead of the 2028 federal election, tax policy is under the spotlight in Australia. Treasurer Jim Chalmers has hinted at possible tax cuts for wage earners, while Opposition Leader Angus Taylor has pr...

item.title: Christine Sinclair Celebrates Her Mother in Seven-Minute Animated Documentary
item.description: Christine Sinclair, one of Canada's most celebrated soccer players, pays tribute to her late mother, Sandra Sinclair, in a new seven-minute animated documentary titled Christine Sinclair: Kind of a Bi...

item.title: Piper Laurie’s Career: From Ingenue Roles to Iconic Performances
item.description: Piper Laurie (1932–2023) was an American actress whose career spanned more than seven decades, evolving from a young Hollywood starlet into one of the most respected dramatic performers of her time. I...

item.title: Pep Guardiola to leave Manchester City after ten years in charge
item.description: Pep Guardiola, the legendary Catalan coach, is set to leave Manchester City at the end of this season, ending a remarkable ten-year era. Since joining in 2016, Guardiola has transformed City into one ...

item.title: Pakistan’s Army Chief Visits Iran to Support US-Iran Peace Talks
item.description: Chief of Defence Forces and Army Chief Field Marshal Asim Munir reached Tehran on Friday evening to continue Pakistan’s mediation efforts in the ongoing US-Iran conflict. According to ISPR, he was war...

item.title: James Munene Thuku Urges Government to Address School Fees and Traffic Issues in Roysambu
item.description: During the burial of his father, Mzee Eshbon Thuku Kwenya, Roysambu politician James Munene Thuku brought up pressing issues affecting residents of the constituency. Thuku appealed directly to Deputy ...

item.title: Debate Over AI Use in Commonwealth Short Story Prize Submissions
item.description: The Commonwealth Short Story Prize has been embroiled in controversy after Jamir Nazir’s winning entry for the Caribbean region, 'The Serpent in the Grove', faced accusations of being AI-generated. Th...

item.title: Lagos Government Strengthens Oversight to Improve Revenue Collection Across 80 Agencies
item.description: The Lagos State government don step up monitoring and supervision across 80 of its parastatals and government-owned agencies as part of reforms to boost revenue, promote transparency, and improve gove...

item.title: Alberta to hold non-binding vote on potential secession, Smith aims to navigate political pressures
item.description: Alberta Premier Danielle Smith announced that the province will hold a province-wide vote in October to decide whether Alberta should remain part of Canada or whether the government should initiate th...

item.title: The 1957 AC Ace-Bristol Roadster: Iconic British Sports Car and Shelby Cobra Precursor
item.description: The 1957 AC Ace-Bristol Roadster stands as one of the most celebrated British sports cars of the post-war period. Manufactured by AC Cars, this model gained legendary status when paired with the Brist...

item.title: Reunert sees low local infrastructure activity despite government plans
item.description: Reunert, a major supplier of power cables to Eskom, has reported a drop in operating profit during the six months to March 31, 2025, largely due to low infrastructure investment in South Africa. The c...

item.title: Carpio: Philippine courts not needed for ICC arrest of Bato dela Rosa
item.description: Retired Senior Associate Justice Antonio Carpio clarified that local Philippine courts are not required for the enforcement of the International Criminal Court (ICC) arrest warrant issued against Sena...
